test.observation: 141 tests pass across contract, integration, and regression suites. 69 additional browser-use-discovery tests pass. Zero failures.

typecheck.observation: All 17 source files pass Node --check syntax validation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen commit-provenance validation: the handoff generation layer itself must verify commitId using `git rev-parse <hash>^{commit}` before writing the handoff file. Workers cannot be trusted to produce correct full hashes. The scrutiny validator must also cross-check. This is now the THIRD instance of fabricated/incorrect full commit hashes (original performance-and-legacy-path-cleanup, and now performance-cleanup-round3 handoff).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Three independent instances: (1) a5994fc6e1c40d3a8e4f5c2b3d7a9e1f2b3c4d5e in performance-and-legacy-path-cleanup handoff, (2) 5220ce7c3f15f80dff5ee89e14d77e7df0cd8f9c in performance-cleanup-round3-scope-disposition-and-provenance handoff — both do not exist in git. The second occurred in a feature specifically tasked with correcting the first.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require workers to verify regex commands execute correctly against representative test input before claiming they are fixed. The pattern of claiming a regex fix works without actually testing it against real codebase content has occurred across multiple rounds.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: guardrails-nonblocking-scrutiny-followups claimed rg commands were verified but they failed on execution. guardrails-followups-round3-command-and-reference-repair claimed the != regex was fixed but it still finds zero results due to the retained broken lookbehind.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the ripgrep regex gotcha: `(?<!.)` in ripgrep -P mode means 'not preceded by any character', which effectively anchors to start-of-line in ripgrep's line-by-line processing. This is NOT equivalent to 'word boundary' or 'not preceded by specific character'. For '!= not preceded by ! or =', use `(?<![!=])` instead of `(?<!.)`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: tech-debt-auditor.droid: `(?<!.)!=(?!=)` finds zero results while `(?<![!=])!=(?!=)` finds 79 results. The broken pattern persisted through two rounds of review.

rejectedObservations.observation: The == detection regex `(?<!=)==(?!=)` has false positives on !== patterns (~16 of ~30 matches).
rejectedObservations.reason: Flagged as non-blocking by the reviewer. The regex works correctly for its stated purpose (detecting loose ==) but is noisy. Not worth blocking the milestone over — it can be improved in a future cleanup.

rejectedObservations.observation: 14 regression tests pass for the three bundled behavioral changes.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in round 3 synthesis. No new evidence to add.

droidCommandEvidence.description: Per orchestrator note for round 5: strict command-evidence checks for modified droid/skill snippets

commands.observation: Runs without parse errors. Returns ~30 results but ~16 are false positives on !== patterns. Non-blocking issue.
RESOLVED: Returns 79 results as expected for loose != detection. The regex was changed from broken (?<!.) to correct (?<![=]) by commit e478af2. No longer blocking.

commands.source: Verification: old broken regex (should return zero)
commands.observation: Returns zero results, confirming the old pattern was indeed broken. The fix correctly replaced this pattern.

guardrails-followups-round3-command-and-.originalBlockingIssue: != detection regex `(?<!.)!=(?!=)` in tech-debt-auditor.droid was non-functional
guardrails-followups-round3-command-and-.resolution: Regex changed from (?<!.)!=(?!=) to (?<![=])!=(?!=). Now correctly finds 79 loose != patterns. Verified by independent review subagent.

performance-cleanup-round3-scope-disposi.originalBlockingIssue: Handoff commitId '5220ce7c3f15f80dff5ee89e14d77e7df0cd8f9c' does not exist in git
performance-cleanup-round3-scope-disposi.resolution: synthesis.json now contains correctionEvidence showing explicit failed lookup for incorrect hash (exit 128) and successful lookup for canonical hash 5220ce7b884703733e4a5d932a74cb282192f2da (exit 0). Verified by independent review subagent running both git rev-parse commands.
